name: Apple'n Oats Streusel Coffeecake
recipeInstructions:
CAKE:
3 cups flour
1 1/2 teaspoons baking powder
1 1/2 teaspoons baking soda
3/4 cup unsalted butter -- softened
1 1/3 cups sugar
3 large eggs
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
1 1/2 cups plain nonfat yogurt
1 medium Granny Smith apple -- peeled and chopped
Oven: 350
Grease two 9x5x3 inch loaf pans.
STREUSEL TOPPING:
Combine first 3 ingredients in a small bowl. Cut in 1/4 cup chilled butter using fingers or a pastry blender until the mixture resembles coarse meal. Set aside.
CAKE:
Sift flour, baking powder, and baking soda into a large bowl. Using an electric mixer, cream together the softened butter and sugar in another large bowl until fluffy. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Mix in vanilla extract.
Fold in dry ingredients alternately with yogurt, beginning and ending with dry ingredients.
Pour about 1-1/2 cups batter into the bottom of each prepared pan. Sprinkle 1/3 cup streusel over each. Sprinkle 1/4 of apple over each.
Divide remaining batter, streusel, and apple between pans, starting with batter. Using a small knife, create swirls in the batter.
Bake until golden brown and a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, about 55 minutes. Cool on racks. Serve warm or at room temperature.
